title: The One with the Cooking Class

jokes.text: That's no way to sell newspapers. Try, 'Extra! Extra! Read all about it!'
jokes.setup: Friend announces bad news
jokes.punchline: Treating the announcement like a newspaper salesman pitch
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection and economy, subverting the expected response to bad news with newspaper vendor energy

jokes.text: I still remember my first good one: 'Everything else in this production of Our Town was simply terrible. Joey Tribbiani was abysmal.'
jokes.setup: Joey claims to remember his first good review
jokes.punchline: The 'good' review actually calls him abysmal
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Joey's optimistic delusion and misunderstanding of reviews. Great structure with the false setup

jokes.text: Reaction beat: Monica arrives to see all the newspapers and assumes they mean good news
jokes.setup: Monica sees newspapers everywhere
jokes.punchline: Her excitement about what she thinks is good press
jokes.explanation: Standard ironic misunderstanding, functional but predictable

jokes.text: But the good news is, no one in a two-block radius will ever know.
jokes.setup: Trying to find silver lining after bad review
jokes.punchline: Extremely limited scope of the 'solution'
jokes.explanation: Decent structure but fairly obvious joke about the futility of the action

jokes.text: What about the rest of Manhattan? - Yeah, they all know.
jokes.setup: Monica asks about the rest of Manhattan
jokes.punchline: Blunt confirmation that everyone else knows
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deadpan delivery, continuing the previous joke

jokes.text: You don't think that, um, 'the chef's mahi-mahi was awful-awful' is bad press?
jokes.setup: Discussion about whether there's no such thing as bad press
jokes.punchline: The rhyming 'awful-awful' describing her mahi-mahi
jokes.explanation: Cute wordplay with the fish name, but fairly light humor

jokes.text: I'm not saying this because I'm your friend, I say it because it's true. Your food is abysmal!
jokes.setup: Friend claims he's being objective, not just supportive
jokes.punchline: Uses the same word from the bad review to 'reassure' her
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection that subverts expectation of comfort, using callback to Joey's review

jokes.text: Funny you should mention diapers.
jokes.setup: Rachel listing baby supplies they need, mentions diaper service
jokes.punchline: Ross needs to use the bathroom right now
jokes.explanation: Bathroom humor setup, functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: I'm just kidding. You can go pee.
jokes.setup: Rachel starts serious conversation about their relationship
jokes.punchline: Reveals she was just messing with desperate Ross
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection playing on audience expectation of serious relationship talk

jokes.text: Sure, I can hang out till I have to meet you.
jokes.setup: Confusion about where they're meeting for movies
jokes.punchline: Joey's nonsensical solution to hang out until he meets himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic - sounds helpful but makes no sense. Good character integration

jokes.text: And people say you don't pay attention.
jokes.setup: Friend thinks Joey already has a job
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic comment about Joey's attention span
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor about Joey's reputation

jokes.text: You cooked him?
jokes.setup: Monica says she's getting revenge on the reviewer
jokes.punchline: Joey's literal interpretation of 'cooking' the critic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey misunderstanding with great economy - two words that completely subvert expectation

jokes.text: You really do live in your own little world, don't you?
jokes.setup: Monica expects front page coverage of her vindication
jokes.punchline: Joey's blunt assessment of her delusion
jokes.explanation: Joey showing rare insight into someone else's behavior, good character moment

jokes.text: We were seeing each other a while ago, but then we were just friends, and then there was one drunken night...
jokes.setup: Explaining relationship status to furniture store clerk
jokes.punchline: Rachel oversharing intimate details to stranger
jokes.explanation: Classic oversharing setup, creates appropriate cringe

jokes.text: Or, yes, stranger, we'd like this delivered, please.
jokes.setup: Rachel oversharing to the delivery clerk
jokes.punchline: Ross cutting off the TMI with pointed 'stranger' reference
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to the oversharing, emphasizing the inappropriateness

jokes.text: That's one of the reasons why we are not a couple.
jokes.setup: Ross excited about dinosaur baby items
jokes.punchline: Rachel citing his paleontology obsession as relationship incompatibility
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor about Ross's nerdy interests being a relationship obstacle

jokes.text: Oh, don't get too worked up over it. It sounds like he's a doctor, but he's not.
jokes.setup: Sales woman impressed by Ross being a paleontologist
jokes.punchline: Rachel deflating the prestige by clarifying he's not a medical doctor
jokes.explanation: Rachel's subtle sabotage of Ross's attempt to impress, good relationship dynamics

jokes.text: Oh, my God. Standing at a cash register, I'm holding a credit card, and I'm bored.
jokes.setup: Ross and saleswoman bonding over paleontology
jokes.punchline: Rachel's blunt expression of boredom in awkward situation
jokes.explanation: Rachel's impatience coming through, relatable shopping experience

jokes.text: A paleontologist who works out. You're like Indiana Jones.
jokes.setup: Saleswoman flirting with Ross about his profession and physique
jokes.punchline: Comparing him to iconic adventurer archaeologist
jokes.explanation: Standard nerdy guy gets compared to cool movie character, predictable but functional

jokes.text: I am like Indiana Jones.
jokes.setup: Being compared to Indiana Jones
jokes.punchline: Ross's pleased acceptance of the comparison
jokes.explanation: Ross's ego boost moment, simple character beat

jokes.text: Ahem, a whore.
jokes.setup: Rachel saying Ross almost got something not on their list
jokes.punchline: Bluntly calling the saleswoman a whore
jokes.explanation: Shocking directness and the theatrical throat clearing, good economy

jokes.text: Oh, my God. Well, the idea-- A woman flirting with a single man? We must alert the church elders.
jokes.setup: Rachel outraged about woman flirting with single Ross
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's sarcastic overreaction about alerting church elders
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's perfect sarcastic takedown of Rachel's irrational jealousy, great escalation with 'church elders'

jokes.text: I mean, here I am, about to pop... and he's out picking up some shopgirl at Sluts-R-Us?
jokes.setup: Rachel explaining why she's upset about Ross flirting
jokes.punchline: Making up the store name 'Sluts-R-Us' as generic slut retailer
jokes.explanation: Creative store name parody, very quotable and shows Rachel's jealous mindset

jokes.text: Is that a real place? Are they hiring?
jokes.setup: Rachel's made-up store name 'Sluts-R-Us'
jokes.punchline: Phoebe treating it as real place and asking about employment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe response - treating absurd premise seriously with her characteristic directness about sex work

jokes.text: Hey, Phoebe. Fatty.
jokes.setup: Chandler greeting Phoebe and pregnant Rachel
jokes.punchline: Calling pregnant Rachel 'Fatty' as casual greeting
jokes.explanation: Chandler's inappropriate but casual insensitivity, creates awkward moment

jokes.text: When I first met you, you were like: [BABBLING] I was like: Shh!
jokes.setup: Chandler asking about first impressions
jokes.punchline: Phoebe demonstrating his nervous babbling and her reaction
jokes.explanation: Physical demonstration makes the critique more vivid and funny

jokes.text: It's just, like, you're trying too hard, always making jokes. You just-- You come off a little needy.
jokes.setup: Explaining Chandler's first impression problems
jokes.punchline: Blunt assessment of his desperation and neediness
jokes.explanation: Phoebe's brutal honesty about Chandler's personality flaws, character-driven

jokes.text: Chandler, I'm not gonna lie to you. But I am gonna run away from you.
jokes.setup: Phoebe promising honesty about whether she liked Chandler initially
jokes.punchline: Subverts expectation by promising to run away instead of answering
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection with perfect setup/payoff structure, very Phoebe-like avoidance tactic

jokes.text: Either eat it or be in it.
jokes.setup: Critic refusing to try her bouillabaisse again
jokes.punchline: Monica threatening to put him in the soup
jokes.explanation: Monica's competitive intensity turning dark, great economy and character moment

jokes.text: I'm torn... between my integrity and my desire to avoid a beating.
jokes.setup: Critic being asked to evaluate Monica's soup under threat
jokes.punchline: Honest assessment of his conflicted position
jokes.explanation: Good acknowledgment of the absurd situation he's in, relatable conflict

jokes.text: Thattgirl! Eh? We should get out of here, there's a new class coming in.
jokes.setup: Critic maintains his negative opinion despite threat
jokes.punchline: Joey proud of the critic's integrity, then practical exit
jokes.explanation: Joey's unexpected appreciation for honesty, then quick scene transition

jokes.text: Monica, you go to the head of the class!
jokes.setup: Monica correctly answering basic cooking question
jokes.punchline: Teacher treating professional chef like star student
jokes.explanation: Ironic since Monica is overqualified for this beginner class

jokes.text: Ah, it must have been obvious, since it was the only thing left in your store.
jokes.setup: Katie saying the delivery forgot an item
jokes.punchline: Ross pointing out it was obviously forgotten since it was the only thing left
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor, Ross being logical

jokes.text: I'm just gonna put this back in my pocket and pretend that didn't happen.
jokes.setup: Katie asks Ross out
jokes.punchline: Ross's awkward attempt to undo the moment
jokes.explanation: Ross's characteristic awkwardness when caught off guard

jokes.text: No, you're a horny bitch! No, you're a horny bitch! No, you're a horny bitch!
jokes.setup: Katie calling herself a horny bitch for asking Ross out
jokes.punchline: Rachel's repeated increasingly aggressive responses
jokes.explanation: Rachel's jealousy manifesting as childish argument escalation

jokes.text: You know, because of Indiana Jones. Not because I'm into S&M.
jokes.setup: Ross mentioning getting his whip
jokes.punchline: Over-explaining to avoid sexual misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Ross's overthinking and over-clarification creating awkwardness

jokes.text: I'm not into anything weird, you know? Heh. Just normal sex.
jokes.setup: Ross trying to clarify he's not into S&M
jokes.punchline: Making it worse by over-sharing about his sex life
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Ross's awkwardness, digging himself deeper

jokes.text: So you had a good day, huh? Big commission, picked up a daddy.
jokes.setup: Katie having successful day at work and asking Ross out
jokes.punchline: Rachel sarcastically referring to Ross as 'daddy'
jokes.explanation: Rachel's jealous sarcasm, reducing Ross to just being a father figure

jokes.text: Oh, and it was great to see you too. And you look fantastic. Although you missed a button.
jokes.setup: Katie being polite to pregnant Rachel
jokes.punchline: Passive-aggressive comment about Rachel's appearance
jokes.explanation: Subtle catty behavior disguised as helpfulness

jokes.text: Hi. Come on in. I'm Regina Phalanges.
jokes.setup: Phoebe role-playing as job interviewer
jokes.punchline: Using her classic fake name 'Regina Phalanges'
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe fake name callback, familiar to fans

jokes.text: Well, you should meet my Uncle Bada.
jokes.setup: Phoebe commenting on Chandler's unusual last name
jokes.punchline: Chandler creating 'Bada Bing' name combination
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay creating the familiar 'Bada Bing' phrase, great economy

jokes.text: I'll let myself out.
jokes.setup: Chandler making a joke during practice interview
jokes.punchline: Phoebe ending the interview immediately
jokes.explanation: Perfect demonstration of Chandler's problem with inappropriate timing

jokes.text: When you say 'use,' do you mean 'eat as a pre-cooking snack'?
jokes.setup: Teacher asking if Monica used cheese in her alfredo
jokes.punchline: Monica admitting she ate the cheese instead of cooking with it
jokes.explanation: Monica's competitive nature undermined by her appetite, good character flaw

jokes.text: Cheese makes me thirsty.
jokes.setup: Monica explaining why she ate the cheese
jokes.punchline: Revealing she also drank the cream
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Monica's complete failure at restraint

jokes.text: I had to ask what it was called when the water makes those bubbles.
jokes.setup: Monica pretending she doesn't know about cooking
jokes.punchline: Claiming she had to ask about boiling water
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of Monica's competitive deception, professional chef pretending total ignorance

jokes.text: I think you'll find if I work here that I don't micromanage. I don't shy away from delegating.
jokes.setup: Chandler describing his management style
jokes.punchline: Only describing what he doesn't do, avoiding what he actually does
jokes.explanation: Chandler's evasive strategy backfiring by highlighting what he avoids rather than his skills

jokes.text: What I do do...
jokes.setup: Interviewer asking Chandler to focus on what he does do
jokes.punchline: Chandler having to say 'do do' which sounds like 'doo doo'
jokes.explanation: Basic toilet humor setup, functional but not sophisticated

jokes.text: Nice sidestep on the 'do do' thing. - Hardest thing I've ever done in my life.
jokes.setup: Interviewer acknowledging Chandler avoided the obvious joke
jokes.punchline: Chandler admitting how difficult it was to resist
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about resisting the obvious joke, perfect for Chandler's character growth

jokes.text: I took the liberty of fashioning a star out of foil.
jokes.setup: Teacher called Monica a star student
jokes.punchline: Monica literally making herself a foil star
jokes.explanation: Monica's competitive desperation manifesting in childish behavior

jokes.text: I know you all hate me, and I'm sorry, but I don't care.
jokes.setup: Monica getting special attention from teacher
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging she's hated but choosing not to care
jokes.explanation: Monica's competitive honesty about her unpopularity but commitment to winning

jokes.text: I got an A? In school? Hey, I'm a dork!
jokes.setup: Joey getting an A grade
jokes.punchline: Excited about being a dork for academic success
jokes.explanation: Joey's genuine excitement about academic achievement, subverting his usual anti-intellectual stance

jokes.text: Oh, relax. It's not a courtroom drama.
jokes.setup: Students gasping at Monica's revelation
jokes.punchline: Teacher deflating the dramatic tension
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of over-dramatic reaction, grounding the scene

jokes.text: But there'll be 30 people under you... so you can dump a certain amount on them.
jokes.setup: Discussing Chandler's management duties
jokes.punchline: Using 'dump' which connects to earlier bathroom humor setup
jokes.explanation: Continuing the bathroom humor theme but feels forced

jokes.text: And then it was really hard to side step that 'duty' thing.
jokes.setup: Chandler reflecting on the interview
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging he avoided the bathroom humor opportunity
jokes.explanation: Meta-acknowledgment of his growth in avoiding immature humor

jokes.text: Duties? Doodies? Poo?
jokes.setup: Chandler finally breaking and making the bathroom jokes he avoided
jokes.punchline: Progressive degradation to just saying 'poo'
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to all the setup, showing Chandler can't help himself even when the stakes are high

jokes.text: Oh, uh-huh, uh-huh, coffee, a little rub-rub-rub under the table?
jokes.setup: Ross saying he and Katie just had coffee
jokes.punchline: Rachel's inappropriate sexual insinuation with physical gesture
jokes.explanation: Rachel's jealousy making her crude and inappropriate

jokes.text: Of course you did. You'd date a gorilla if it called you Indiana Jones.
jokes.setup: Ross saying he liked Katie
jokes.punchline: Rachel's assessment of Ross's ego weakness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character insight - Rachel perfectly identifying Ross's specific weakness for flattery

jokes.text: Did you get a fresh batch of pregnancy hormones today?
jokes.setup: Rachel being irrationally jealous
jokes.punchline: Ross attributing it to pregnancy hormones
jokes.explanation: Standard pregnancy hormone joke, functional but predictable

jokes.text: I want you to be at my constant beck and call, 24 hours a day. I'm very sorry, but that is just the way that I feel.
jokes.setup: Rachel explaining her unreasonable demands
jokes.punchline: Her honest admission of complete selfishness
jokes.explanation: Rachel's brutal honesty about her selfishness is refreshing and relatable for pregnancy

jokes.text: You're pressing the baby into my bladder... and now I have to pee. Sorry.
jokes.setup: Ross hugging pregnant Rachel
jokes.punchline: Physical consequence of the hug on pregnant body
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy of pregnancy, callback to earlier bathroom theme

jokes.text: Oh, Rach, I was just messing around. Heh.
jokes.setup: Rachel seeming to consider something more with Ross
jokes.punchline: Ross reveals he was just joking like she did earlier
jokes.explanation: Nice callback structure mirroring Rachel's earlier fake-out

jokes.text: The first A I've gotten since 7th grade and I didn't have to sleep with the teacher.
jokes.setup: Joey celebrating his cooking class success
jokes.punchline: Implying he's slept with teachers for grades before
jokes.explanation: Dark implication about Joey's past academic 'success' methods, fits his character

jokes.text: Yeah, this was a stupid idea.
jokes.setup: Teacher asking basic acting question Joey can't answer
jokes.punchline: Joey immediately realizing his overconfidence was misplaced
jokes.explanation: Quick deflation of Joey's brief academic confidence, good ending beat

tmdb_overview: Monica's restaurant receives a negative review. Rachel becomes jealous watching Ross interact with another woman.
